meta.title: BMW N54/N55 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost

hero.h1: BMW N54/N55 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost
hero.subHeadline: The BMW N54/N55 is a legendary 3.0-litre turbo six – brilliant when sorted, but high‑pressure fuel pumps and injectors are well‑known weak points. Backed by real UK enquiry data [ESTIMATED].

verdictRating.confidence: 88% – based on cross‑referenced specialist reports and UK owner surveys.

rows.score: 12 (Many common failures (HPFP, injectors, coils) are replaceable, but wastegate rattle often requires turbo overhaul or replacement – labour‑intensive)
16 (Excellent OEM and aftermarket support; HPFP and injectors are widely available, though index‑12 injectors are pricey)
12 (Recon cost ~45–60% of lowest vehicle value (e.g. older E90 335i) → 12)

verdictRating.oneLineVerdict: The N54/N55 is a tuner’s dream with huge potential — but HPFP, injector and wastegate issues are common — buy only with full service history and recent injector codes.
verdictRating.bestFor: Enthusiasts who appreciate effortless power and are comfortable with proactive maintenance.
verdictRating.avoidIf: You can’t afford the running costs of a high‑performance turbo six, or you expect Toyota‑like reliability without attention.

compatibility.crossBrandNote: Exclusive to BMW – no cross‑brand fitment.

costGuide.labourEstimate: Fitting an N54/N55 typically takes 10–14 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [ESTIMATED].

items.question: How much does an N54/N55 engine replacement cost?
items.answer: A reconditioned N54/N55 typically costs £3,500–£5,500 supply only [ESTIMATED], varying by exact variant and year. Fitting adds £650–£1,300 depending on labour rates. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

items.question: Is the BMW N54/N55 a reliable engine?
items.answer: We rate the N54/N55 at 3.5 stars out of 5, based on an 88% confidence level from specialist reports and UK owner surveys [ESTIMATED]. The core engine is strong, but peripheral items (HPFP, injectors, wastegates) are regular failure points.

items.question: What is the most common N54/N55 failure?
items.answer: The high‑pressure fuel pump (HPFP) fails prematurely – often between 60,000–100,000 miles – causing long cranks and loss of power. Injectors (especially index 9/10) also leak and need replacement at similar intervals.

items.question: Which BMW models use the N54/N55?
items.answer: The N54/N55 powered the 135i, 335i, 535i, X3 35i, X5 35i, Z4 sDrive35i, and 740i, across multiple generations. It was BMW’s premier turbo six for over a decade.

items.question: How long does the N54/N55 last?
items.answer: With proper maintenance (regular oil changes, timely HPFP/injector replacement, and attention to cooling), the N54/N55 can easily exceed 150,000 miles. Many high‑mileage examples remain on the road with original engine internals.
